item.title: Police arrest 18-year-old suspect for allegedly running at U.S. Capitol with shotgun
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police arrested a person with "what appears to be a gun" near the Capitol, according to an email sent out to congressional offices. W...

item.title: After Super Bowl ad, consumers rethink the tradeoffs of home surveillance tech
item.description: Sentiment around widely used home surveillance tools is souring as high-profile cases reveal just how deeply law enforcement can tap the data they gen...

item.title: Zelensky says Ukrainian public won't let him hand Russia territory
item.description: The Ukrainian people would reject a peace deal that involves Ukraine unilaterally withdrawing from the eastern Donbas region and turning it over to Ru...

item.title: U.S. and Iran say progress made in Geneva nuclear talks
item.description: The U.S.-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva on Tuesday "made progress," but "there are still a lot of details to discuss," a U.S. official told Axios. The l...

item.title: "The subpoenas are coming," bipartisan firm warns corporate America
item.description: Matthew Miller and Tucker Eskew — veterans of high-profile campaigns, now partners at Vianovo, a bipartisan management and communications firm — write...
